src: τοῦτο ἐκ. μακροῦ προοικονομεῖται,
lit: This is prearranged from long ago,
sm: This is preordained from long ago,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ἐκ. μακροῦ -> ἐκ μακροῦ.

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the purpose/result construction follows in lines 432-433.
syn.svo: Subject: τοῦτο. Verb: προοικονομεῖται. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ μακροῦ.
syn.rhet: The mystery is framed as long foreordained.

src: ἵν᾽ ὁπόταν γένηται πίστεως τύχῃ
lit: In order that, whenever it comes to pass, it may obtain belief
sm: so that, whenever it comes to pass, it may win belief

syn.mc: Purpose clause with an embedded temporal condition.
syn.sub: ἵνα introduces purpose; ὁπόταν introduces the temporal frame within it.
syn.svo: Subject: implied τοῦτο / τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Verbs: γένηται, τύχῃ. Object/complement with τυγχάνω: πίστεως.
syn.rhet: The foreordination is justified by later recognizability and faith.

src: ἐς μακροῦ προοραθέν.
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg n
lit: Having been foreseen from long ago.
sm: having been foreseen from long ago.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ἐς μακροῦ -> ἐκ μακροῦ.

syn.mc: Participial phrase completing the thought of lines 431-432.
syn.svo: Participle: προοραθέν, agreeing with the implied neuter subject τοῦτο / μυστήριον. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ μακροῦ.
syn.ptc: προοραθέν explains how the mystery can later obtain faith: it was foreseen beforehand.
syn.rhet: The preordination/foreseeing pair tightens providential teleology.

src: οὕτω δὴ καὶ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον
lit: Thus indeed also the mystery of the Lord
sm: Thus indeed the mystery of the Lord also

syn.mc: Subject phrase introducing the analogy drawn from lines 431-433.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the sentence continues through line 438.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Adverbs: οὕτω δὴ καί.
syn.rhet: Analogy marker transitions from general principle to christological application.

src: ἐκ μακροῦ προτυπωθέν,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg n
lit: Having been prefigured from long ago,
sm: having been prefigured from long ago,

syn.mc: Participial modifier of the subject in line 434.
syn.svo: Participle: προτυπωθέν, agreeing with τὸ ... μυστήριον. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ μακροῦ.
syn.ptc: προτυπωθέν is circumstantial, first in a chain of descriptive participles.
syn.rhet: Prefiguration becomes the specifically typological form of foreseeing.

src: διὰ δὲ τύπον ὁραθέν,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg n
lit: And having been seen through a type,
sm: and having been seen through a type,

syn.mc: Second participial modifier of the subject in line 434.
syn.svo: Participle: ὁραθέν, agreeing with μυστήριον. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τύπον.
syn.ptc: ὁραθέν is circumstantial, coordinating with προτυπωθέν.
syn.rhet: The mystery is both prefigured beforehand and visually apprehended through type.

src: σήμερον πίστεως τυγχάνει τετελεσμένον
tok: pf mp ptcp nom sg n
lit: Today, having been accomplished, it obtains belief
sm: today, once accomplished, it wins belief

syn.mc: Main clause of the application sentence.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle is predicative with the subject.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον, understood from line 434. Verb: τυγχάνει. Genitive complement: πίστεως. Predicative participle: τετελεσμένον.
syn.ptc: τετελεσμένον is predicative with the subject, indicating the state in which it obtains belief.
syn.rhet: The movement is from foreseen and typified to presently fulfilled and believed.

src: καίτοι ὡς καινὸν τοῖς ἀνθρώποις νομιζόμενον.
tok: pres mp ptcp nom/acc sg n
lit: And yet being regarded by men as new.
sm: and yet being regarded by men as something new.

syn.mc: Concessive participial phrase modifying the mystery.
syn.svo: Participle: νομιζόμενον, agreeing with μυστήριον. Dative of reference/agent-like complement: τοῖς ἀνθρώποις. Predicative complement: ὡς καινόν.
syn.ptc: νομιζόμενον is concessive: though fulfilled and πιστόν, it is still thought new.
syn.rhet: The paradox of ancient-yet-new is reintroduced.

src: ἔστιν γὰρ καινὸν καὶ παλαιὸν τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον"
ana: ἔστιν γὰρ καινὸν καὶ παλαιὸν τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον·
lit: For the mystery of the Lord is new and old.
sm: For the mystery of the Lord is both new and old.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Main explanatory clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; lines 440-441 unpack the pair.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Verb: ἔστιν. Predicate adjectives: καινὸν καὶ παλαιόν.
syn.rhet: Compressed paradox restates a major refrain of the work.

src: παλαιὸν μὲν κατὰ τὸν νόμον,
lit: Old, on the one hand, according to the law,
sm: old, on the one hand, according to the law,

syn.mc: Elliptical explanatory phrase unpacking line 439.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: παλαιόν. Prepositional phrase: κατὰ τὸν νόμον.
syn.rhet: First half of the old/new explanatory antithesis.

src: καινὸν δὲ κατὰ τὴν χάριν.
lit: But new according to grace.
sm: but new according to grace.

syn.mc: Elliptical explanatory phrase paired with line 440.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: καινόν. Prepositional phrase: κατὰ τὴν χάριν.
syn.rhet: Second half of the old/new antithesis.

src: ἀλλ᾽ ἐὰν ἀποβλέψῃς εἰς τὸν τύπον,
lit: But if you look toward the type,
sm: But if you look to the type,

syn.mc: Conditional subordinate clause.
syn.sub: ἐὰν introduces the protasis; the apodosis is in line 445.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ἀποβλέψῃς. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τὸν τύπον.
syn.rhet: The audience is invited into typological seeing.

src: τοῦτον ὄψῃ διὰ τῆς ἐκβάσεως.
lit: You will see this one through the outcome.
sm: you will see this through the outcome.

syn.mc: Apodosis of the condition in line 444.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the completed condition.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ὄψῃ. Object: τοῦτον. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τῆς ἐκβάσεως.
syn.rhet: Interpretive knowledge comes by seeing the end from the type.

src: τοιγαροῦν εἰ βούλει τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον ἰδέσθαι,
lit: Therefore then, if you wish to see the mystery of the Lord,
sm: Therefore, if you wish to see the mystery of the Lord,

syn.mc: Main inferential lead-in with conditional clause.
syn.sub: εἰ introduces the condition; the imperative apodosis begins in line 447.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: βούλει. Infinitive complement: ἰδέσθαι. Object of infinitive: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον.
syn.rhet: The discourse turns exhortative and demonstrative.

src: ἀπόβλεψον εἰς τὸν Ἀβὲλ τὸν ὁμοίως φονευόμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: Look toward Abel, the one being killed in like manner,
sm: Look to Abel, who was killed in like manner,

syn.mc: Imperative main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle is attributive to Ἀβέλ.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀπόβλεψον. Object: τὸν Ἀβέλ. Attributive phrase: τὸν ὁμοίως φονευόμενον.
syn.rhet: First in a typological catalogue of biblical figures.

src: εἰς τὸν ᾿Ισὰκ τὸν ὁμοίως συμποδιζόμενον,
ana: εἰς τὸν Ἰσὰκ τὸν ὁμοίως συμποδιζόμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: Toward Isaac, the one being likewise bound,
sm: to Isaac, who was likewise bound,
emd: Normalized stray spacing psili in ᾿Ισὰκ -> Ἰσὰκ for analysis.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the imperative catalogue begun in line 447.
syn.svo: Object under the carried imperative ἀπόβλεψον: τὸν Ἰσάκ. Attributive participle: συμποδιζόμενον.
syn.rhet: Second typological figure in the catalogue.

src: εἰς τὸν ᾿Ιωσὴφ τὸν ὁμοίως πιπρασκόμενον,
ana: εἰς τὸν Ἰωσὴφ τὸν ὁμοίως πιπρασκόμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: Toward Joseph, the one being likewise sold,
sm: to Joseph, who was likewise sold,
emd: Normalized stray spacing psili in ᾿Ιωσὴφ -> Ἰωσὴφ for analysis.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the imperative catalogue.
syn.svo: Object under the carried imperative ἀπόβλεψον: τὸν Ἰωσήφ. Attributive participle: πιπρασκόμενον.
syn.rhet: Third typological figure extends the chain of prefigurations.

src: εἰς τὸν Μωυσέα τὸν ὁμοίως ἐκτιθέμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: Toward Moses, the one being likewise exposed,
sm: to Moses, who was likewise exposed,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the imperative catalogue.
syn.svo: Object under the carried imperative ἀπόβλεψον: τὸν Μωυσέα. Attributive participle: ἐκτιθέμενον.
syn.rhet: The catalogue sweeps across patriarchal and Mosaic history.

src: εἰς τὸν Δαυεὶδ τὸν ὁμοίως διωκόμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg m
lit: Toward David, the one being likewise pursued,
sm: to David, who was likewise pursued,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the imperative catalogue.
syn.svo: Object under the carried imperative ἀπόβλεψον: τὸν Δαυείδ. Attributive participle: διωκόμενον.
syn.rhet: The typological chain now includes royal persecution.

src: εἰς τοὺς προφήτας τοὺς ὁμοίως διὰ Χριστὸν πάσχοντας.
tok: pres act ptcp acc pl m
lit: Toward the prophets, the ones likewise suffering on account of Christ.
sm: to the prophets, who likewise suffered on account of Christ.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the imperative catalogue.
syn.svo: Object under the carried imperative ἀπόβλεψον: τοὺς προφήτας. Attributive participle: πάσχοντας. Prepositional phrase: διὰ Χριστόν.
syn.ptc: πάσχοντας is attributive to the prophets.
syn.rhet: The catalogue culminates in the prophetic witness directly linked to Christ.

src: ἀπόβλεψον δὲ καὶ εἰς τὸ ἐν γῇ Αἰγύπτῳ πρόβατον σφαζόμενον,
tok: pres mp ptcp acc sg n
lit: But look also toward the sheep being slaughtered in the land of Egypt,
sm: But look also to the sheep being slaughtered in the land of Egypt,

syn.mc: Renewed imperative main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; lines 454-455 continue its attributive elaboration.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀπόβλεψον. Object: τὸ ... πρόβατον. Prepositional phrase: ἐν γῇ Αἰγύπτῳ. Attributive participle: σφαζόμενον.
syn.rhet: The catalogue returns from human types to the paschal animal itself.

src: τὸ πάταξαν τὴν Αἴγυπτον
tok: aor act ptcp acc sg n
lit: The one having struck Egypt
sm: the one that struck Egypt

syn.mc: Attributive participial phrase continuing the object of line 453.
syn.svo: Article plus participle τὸ πάταξαν modifies πρόβατον. Object of participle: τὴν Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The lamb is described by its efficacy in striking Egypt.

src: καὶ σῶσαν τὸν ᾿Ισραὴλ διὰ τοῦ αἵματος.
ana: καὶ σῶσαν τὸν Ἰσραὴλ διὰ τοῦ αἵματος.
tok: aor act ptcp acc sg n
lit: And having saved Israel through the blood.
sm: and that saved Israel through the blood.
emd: Normalized stray spacing psili in ᾿Ισραὴλ -> Ἰσραὴλ for analysis.

syn.mc: Second attributive participial phrase continuing line 454.
syn.svo: Participle: σῶσαν, attributive to πρόβατον. Object: τὸν Ἰσραήλ. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τοῦ αἵματος.
syn.ptc: σῶσαν is attributive and coordinate with πάταξαν.
syn.rhet: The lamb is summed up by judgment on Egypt and salvation for Israel.

src: Ἔστιν δὲ διὰ προφητικῆς φωνῆς τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον κηρυσ-
ana: Ἔστιν δὲ διὰ προφητικῆς φωνῆς τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον κηρυσσόμενον.
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg n
lit: And the mystery of the Lord is being proclaimed through prophetic voice.
sm: And the mystery of the Lord is proclaimed through prophetic utterance.
emd: The raw line is hyphenated at κηρυσ- and completed by stray `[σόμενον` at the start of line 458; analysis reconstructs the intended κηρυσσόμενον.

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the citation formula follows in line 458.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Verb: ἔστιν with participial complement κηρυσσόμενον. Prepositional phrase: διὰ προφητικῆς φωνῆς.
syn.rhet: The discourse pivots from typological exempla to prophetic testimony.

src: φησὶν γὰρ Μωυσῆς πρὸς τὸν λαόν" [σόμενον.
ana: φησὶν γὰρ Μωυσῆς πρὸς τὸν λαόν·
lit: For Moses says to the people:
sm: For Moses says to the people:
emd: The raw line contains both a stray OCR quote and the carried-over `[σόμενον` from the hyphenated word of line 457. That fragment is assigned to line 457's reconstruction, leaving this normal citation formula.

syn.mc: Main citation formula.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the quoted speech begins in line 459.
syn.svo: Subject: Μωυσῆς. Verb: φησίν. Prepositional phrase: πρὸς τὸν λαόν.
syn.rhet: Standard prophetic citation introduces scriptural proof.

src: Καὶ ὄψεσθε τὴν ζωὴν ὑμῶν κρεμαμένην
tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg f
lit: And you will see your life hanging
sm: And you will see your life hanging

syn.mc: Quoted main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the sentence continues in line 460.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: ὄψεσθε. Object: τὴν ζωὴν ὑμῶν. Predicative participle: κρεμαμένην.
syn.rhet: The citation introduces a stark visual image of threatened life.

src: ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν ὑμῶν νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας,
lit: Before your eyes by night and by day,
sm: before your eyes night and day,

syn.mc: Prepositional/adverbial continuation of line 459.
syn.svo: Adverbial modifiers of κρεμαμένην / ὄψεσθε: ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν ὑμῶν, νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας.
syn.rhet: The prophecy intensifies by making the danger constant and visible.
